text: Amazon.com, Inc. operates through three primary segments: North America, International, and Amazon Web Services (AWS) [43e13619-4c35-4dd4-a32b-ceaa18319a42]. The company engages in retail sale of consumer products and subscriptions through online and physical stores, offering merchandise purchased for resale alongside products from third-party sellers [43e13619-4c35-4dd4-a32b-ceaa18319a42]. Beyond retail, Amazon manufactures electronic devices including Kindle, Fire tablets, Fire TVs, Ring, Blink, eero, and Echo, while also developing media content [43e13619-4c35-4dd4-a32b-ceaa18319a42]. The company provides compute, storage, database, analytics, machine learning, and other cloud services through AWS, alongside fulfillment, advertising, and digital content subscriptions [43e13619-4c35-4dd4-a32b-ceaa18319a42]. Amazon Prime membership program serves as a key customer retention mechanism [43e13619-4c35-4dd4-a32b-ceaa18319a42]. The company serves consumers, sellers, developers, enterprises, content creators, and advertisers across North America and internationally [43e13619-4c35-4dd4-a32b-ceaa18319a42]. As of the reporting period, Amazon employs approximately 1,560,000 full-time employees under CEO Andrew R. Jassy [e65da658-2e8e-45cc-a6fc-d7c488c49e6a].
